Application.description:
A SAPI application

Applications are the top-level container in SAPI's hierarchy: Application -> Service -> Instance

master.description: Whether this is a master record (from remote datacenter)

CreateApplicationBody.description: Request body for creating an application

master.description: Whether this is a master record (from remote datacenter)

uuid.description: Application UUID (optional, auto-generated if not provided)

CreateInstanceBody.description: Request body for creating an instance

master.description: Whether this is a master record (from remote datacenter)

uuid.description: Instance UUID (optional, auto-generated if not provided)

CreateManifestBody.description: Request body for creating a manifest

master.description: Whether this is a master record (from remote datacenter)

path.description: Path where the rendered config file is placed (required)

post_cmd_linux.description: Command to run after rendering on Linux

template.description: Template content (required; can be a string or JSON object)

uuid.description: Manifest UUID (optional, auto-generated if not provided)

CreateServiceBody.description: Request body for creating a service

master.description: Whether this is a master record (from remote datacenter)

uuid.description: Service UUID (optional, auto-generated if not provided)

Instance.description:
A SAPI instance

Instances are the leaf nodes of SAPI's hierarchy: Application -> Service -> Instance

An instance typically maps to a VM or agent zone.

job_uuid.description: Job UUID (only present on async create)

master.description: Whether this is a master record

LogLevelResponse.description:
Response for GET /loglevel

Bunyan's `log.level()` returns an integer (e.g., 30 for "info"), so `level` is a generic JSON value rather than a string.

Manifest.description:
A SAPI configuration manifest

Manifests define templates that are rendered with metadata to produce configuration files for instances.

master.description: Whether this is a master record

path.description: Path where the rendered config file is placed in the zone

post_cmd.description: Command to run after rendering the template

post_cmd_linux.description: Command to run after rendering on Linux (lx-branded zones)

template.description: Template content (can be a string template or a JSON object)

PingResponse.description:
Response for GET /ping

Note: PingResponse uses a mix of snake_case and camelCase field names. `storType` and `storAvailable` are camelCase in the wire format.

storAvailable.description: Whether the storage backend is available

Service.description:
A SAPI service

Services belong to an application and contain instances. Application -> Service -> Instance

master.description: Whether this is a master record

ServiceType.description:
Service/instance type

Distinguishes between VM-based services and agent services. Agents are only visible in API v2+.

oneOf.description: Agent service (only visible in API v2+)

level.description: Log level to set (string name or integer)

StorageType.description:
SAPI storage backend type

Returned by GET /ping as `storType`. The value is the JavaScript constructor name of the storage backend.

UpdateAction.description:
Action for update endpoints (applications, services, instances)

Controls how attributes (`params`, `metadata`, `manifests`, etc.) are modified. Default action is `update` when not specified.

UpdateApplicationBody.description:
Request body for updating an application

The `action` field controls how attributes are modified: - `update` (default): merge changes into existing attributes - `replace`: replace entire attribute sections wholesale - `delete`: delete specified keys from attributes

UpdateInstanceBody.description:
Request body for updating an instance

The `action` field controls how attributes are modified: - `update` (default): merge changes into existing attributes - `replace`: replace entire attribute sections wholesale - `delete`: delete specified keys from attributes

UpdateServiceBody.description:
Request body for updating a service

The `action` field controls how attributes are modified: - `update` (default): merge changes into existing attributes - `replace`: replace entire attribute sections wholesale - `delete`: delete specified keys from attributes

UpgradeInstanceBody.description: Request body for upgrading an instance image

info.description: Triton SAPI - Services and Configuration API for managing applications, services, instances, and configuration manifests
info.title: Triton SAPI

get.description: Returns all applications, optionally filtered by name or owner.

parameters.description: Include master records from remote datacenter
parameters.name: include_master

post.description: Creates a new application. Requires `name` and `owner_uuid`.

delete.description: Deletes an application by UUID. Returns 204 on success.

get.description: Returns a single application by UUID.

put.description: Updates application attributes. The `action` body field controls how attributes are modified (update/replace/delete). Default is "update".

post.description: Forces a synchronization of cached data. Returns 204 on success.

get.description:
Returns the assembled configuration (manifests + metadata) for a specific instance. The response is freeform JSON since it depends on the instance's application/service/instance configuration hierarchy.

The original Node.js endpoint also sets an ETag header for conditional request support.
get.summary: Get assembled config for an instance

get.description: Returns all instances, optionally filtered by service or type.

parameters.description: Include master records from remote datacenter
parameters.name: include_master

post.description: Creates a new instance. Requires `service_uuid`. If `async=true` query parameter is set, returns immediately with a `job_uuid` instead of waiting for provisioning to complete.

parameters.description: If true, return immediately with a job_uuid instead of waiting for provisioning to complete. Default: false.
parameters.name: async

delete.description: Deletes an instance by UUID. Returns 204 on success.

get.description: Returns a single instance by UUID.

put.description: Updates instance attributes. The `action` body field controls how attributes are modified (update/replace/delete). Default is "update".

get.description: Returns the assembled zone parameters for provisioning. This is the merged result of application, service, and instance params/metadata.

put.description: Upgrades an instance to a new image. Requires `image_uuid` in the body.

parameters.description: Include master records from remote datacenter
parameters.name: include_master

post.description: Creates a new configuration manifest. Requires `name`, `path`, and `template`.

delete.description: Deletes a manifest by UUID. Returns 204 on success.

get.description: Returns a single manifest by UUID.
Returns the current SAPI mode ("proto" or "full").

post.description: Transitions SAPI to the specified mode. Only "full" is accepted (transition from proto to full mode).

get.description: Returns SAPI status including operating mode and storage availability.
Returns all services, optionally filtered by name, application, or type.

parameters.description: Include master records from remote datacenter
parameters.name: include_master

post.description: Creates a new service. Requires `name` and `application_uuid`.

delete.description: Deletes a service by UUID. Returns 204 on success.

get.description: Returns a single service by UUID.

put.description: Updates service attributes. The `action` body field controls how attributes are modified (update/replace/delete). Default is "update".
